Title: IRB extends tax filing deadline to May 15
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Taxpayers now have until May 15 to submit their Tax Return Form (BN) for the Year of Assessment 2025, following a 15-day extension granted by the Inland Revenue Board of Malaysia (IRB).
IRB said the extension was given in line with the mandatory e-Filing system, allowing taxpayers more time to ensure accurate and complete submissions.
The original deadline for individuals and non-individuals – including organisations, estates and Hindu joint families not carrying on a business – was set for tomorrow.
"Taxpayers are advised to make full use of this extension by filing early to ensure a smooth process and avoid system congestion near the deadline," IRB said in a statement.
It stressed that compliance with the stipulated deadline is essential to avoid action under the Income Tax Act 1967, including penalties for late submission or failure to file.
The e-Filing service can be accessed via the MyTax portal or through the official IRB website.
Taxpayers are also encouraged to refer to the relevant BN explanatory notes if clarification is needed before submission.
"They are also advised to review all tax-related documents and information in advance, including income statements, receipts and supporting records.
"Tax returns should be completed carefully to ensure submissions are accurate and complete," the statement said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Don't withdraw reports': Nancy urges abuse victims
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri has urged victims of domestic violence not to be afraid and not to withdraw their police reports.
The Women, Family and Community Development minister expressed disappointment over those who retract their reports, noting that the ministry provides shelters and assistance to support victims.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump: Iran 'better get smart soon'
Article:
WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump on Wednesday warned Iran to "better get smart soon" as efforts by Washington and Teheran to end hostilities appeared at a standstill.
"Iran can't get their act together. They don't know how to sign a nonnuclear deal. They better get smart soon!" the US leader posted on his Truth Social platform, alongside an illustration of Trump holding an assault rifle, with the caption "NO MORE MR. NICE GUY!" — AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Court hears RM19.5mil to Bersatu came with donation documents
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Banker's cheques totalling RM19.5 million deposited into Bersatu's account between 2021 and 2022 came with supporting documents describing the sums as donations, the High Court heard today.
Bank Muamalat assistant manager at the PKNS Shah Alam branch, Norazeanti Miswan, 39, said the matter during cross-examination by Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin's lawyer Datuk Amer Hamzah Arshad.
She was testifying in the case against the former prime minister, who faces seven charges linked to the Jana Wibawa project.
It was earlier established during the trial that the political party had received the monies from Mamfor Sdn Bhd's account between May 12, 2021 and Nov 16, 2022.
Norazeanti also agreed with the lawyer's contention that the accompanying letters were addressed to Bersatu headquarters and directed to the party's treasurer.
She said all documents relating to the transactions were submitted to the bank as supporting records and retained by the institution.
Amer Hamzah:
So these banker's cheques and letters clearly show the payments from Mamfor to Bersatu were transparent donations?
Norazeanti:
Agreed.
Amer Hamzah:
The donations were made to Bersatu, not to Muhyiddin personally?
Norazeanti:
Agreed.
Amer Hamzah:
All transactions were recorded in the banking system?
Norazeanti:
Agreed.
Amer Hamzah:
So it is not possible to conceal such transactions within the system?
Norazeanti:
Agreed.
Norazeanti in her witness statement said the RM19.5 million was deposited into the party's account through 29 transactions carried out regularly during that period.
She said each transaction triggered a Suspicious Transaction Report (STR), which was escalated to the bank's headquarters for further review.
Under re-examination by deputy public prosecutor Mahadi Abdul Jumaat, she said STRs were raised for almost all transactions involving Bersatu due to concerns over their nature.
Norazeanti said the use of banker's cheques in such a manner was unusual and not previously encountered in her banking experience.
Muhyiddin, 78, is facing four charges of using his position to obtain RM225.3 million in gratification linked to the Jana Wibawa project from several entities.
He is also facing three charges of receiving RM200 million in proceeds from unlawful activities, allegedly deposited into Bersatu's accounts at AmBank and CIMB Bank between February 2021 and July 2022.
The trial before Judge Noor Ruwena Md Nurdin resumes on May 25.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ministry wants list of domestic abusers
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Women, Family and Community Development Ministry is seeking cooperation from relevant authorities to obtain a list of domestic violence offenders.
Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri said the move forms part of broader efforts to identify systemic weaknesses that adversely affect victims and to implement improvements.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pahang MB wants return of three Malaysian elephants from Japan
Article:
KUANTAN: Pahang Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail wants Zoo Taiping & Night Safari to take immediate action to bring back three Malaysian elephants – Dara, Amoi and Kelat – currently housed at Tennoji Zoo in Osaka, Japan.
He said the elephants should be returned to Malaysia and placed at the Kuala Gandah National Elephant Conservation Centre in Temerloh, which he described as more suitable for their natural habitat and needs.
He said the conservation centre was equipped with the necessary expertise and offered a more conducive environment for elephant care and preservation, particularly in terms of social interaction, freedom of movement and psychological well-being.
"We have a responsibility to ensure that every national treasure is properly preserved, and in this case, the most appropriate place for them is back on home soil," he said in a statement today.
Wan Rosdy said that the matter should not be taken lightly, as the elephants form part of the country's biodiversity heritage and deserve the highest level of protection.
"Based on calls from the Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup, as well as various parties including non-governmental organisations (NGOs) and members of the public regarding the condition of the elephants, I am of the view that this matter cannot be overlooked," he said.
Earlier, Arthur said that proposals from various parties, including NGOs, to bring back the three elephants should be given
serious consideration
.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sabah rules out unlicensed crocodile hunting, cites legal limits
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: Sabah cannot permit unlicensed crocodile hunting at present as it would contravene existing laws and could jeopardise ecosystem sustainability, says state Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Datuk Jafry Ariffin.
He said the crocodile species found in Sabah, Crocodylus porosus, was listed under Schedule 2, Part I, which classifies it as a protected animal under the Wildlife Conservation Enactment 1997.
At international level, the species is listed under Appendix I of the Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora (CITES).
"Therefore, Sabah is unable to allow unlicensed crocodile hunting for now as it would contravene existing legal provisions and could jeopardise ecosystem sustainability.
"However, for Sabah and Sarawak, the species has been downlisted to Appendix II with CITES approval to allow regulated crocodile farming and export activities.
"This reclassification is the result of effective crocodile management plans implemented in Sabah," he said in his winding-up speech at the state legislative assembly sitting.
Jafry said the ministry was implementing a comprehensive approach through integrated operations, issuance of hunting licences to control population levels, awareness programmes, installation of warning signage, scientific studies, and pilot projects in Batu Puteh, Kinabatangan.
Based on 2025 data, Sabah recorded 11 fatalities and three injury cases due to crocodile attacks.
Most incidents occurred in oil palm plantation areas during fishing activities.
Two of the fatalities were in Kinabatangan involving foreign workers.
"The licensing system is designed to regulate hunting activities and the trade of crocodile-based products such as skins and meat, while curbing uncontrolled exploitation and illegal sales," he said.
On human-elephant conflict, Jafry said the Sabah Wildlife Department had implemented measures including GPS tracking of elephant movements, installation of electric fencing in hotspot areas, and establishment and training of elephant response teams involving local communities.
He added that public awareness programmes were ongoing, alongside collaboration with stakeholders through pilot projects involving the planting of elephant food sources to reduce encroachment into residential areas.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Woman claims secret filming in JB hotpot restaurant toilet, police hunt suspect
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A woman has lodged a police report after alleging she was secretly filmed while using a restroom at a hotpot restaurant along Jalan Trus here yesterday.
The woman shared a
screenshot of the police report on Facebook,
claiming the incident occurred after midnight.
She alleged that a mobile phone was pushed under a cubicle partition from an adjacent stall while she was inside the toilet.
Startled, she said she shouted and kicked the partition but was unable to pursue the suspect as her skirt was not fully fastened.
By the time she exited the cubicle, the suspect – believed to be a man – had already fled.
She claimed she alerted restaurant staff but alleged that the initial response lacked urgency.
The restaurant manager allegedly declined to allow her to view closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage, citing company policy, but confirmed that a man had been seen heading towards the women's restroom during the time frame.
Meanwhile, Johor Baru (South) police chief Assistant Commissioner Raub Selamat confirmed that a report had been lodged and that investigations are ongoing to identify the suspect.
He said that no arrests had been made.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Open burning triggers 1.6ha peat forest fire in Rantau Panjang
Article:
RANTAU PANJANG: A peat forest fire covering 1.6 hectares broke out along Jalan Pasir Mas-Rantau Panjang today, believed to have been caused by open burning.
No casualties were reported in the blaze, which occurred opposite the Prima housing area.
Rantau Panjang Fire and Rescue Department operations chief Ahmad Nasaruddin Mahussain said seven personnel were dispatched to the scene after receiving an emergency call at 12.45pm.
He said it took more than an hour to bring the fire under control in over half of the affected area.
"As of 2pm, firefighting operations are still ongoing to extinguish the remaining 0.8 hectares. Thick smoke from the fire has reduced visibility in the area," he said.
Motorists are advised to exercise caution and drive slowly when using the route.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain to hit KL, Putrajaya, eight states until 7pm
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Heavy rain, thunderstorms and strong winds are forecast to hit the capital, Putrajaya and eight other states until 7pm today.
The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia), in a statement, said the weather was expected to affect all of Perlis, as well as several areas in Kedah, including Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Pokok Sena, Padang Terap, Pendang, Kuala Muda, Sik, Baling and Kulim.
In Penang, the affected areas are Seberang Perai Utara and Seberang Perai Tengah, while in Pahang, the warning covers Jerantut, Bentong, Temerloh, Maran, Kuantan and Bera.
In Selangor, the affected districts include Kuala Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat and Sepang.
In Johor, similar conditions are expected in Batu Pahat, Kluang, Pontian, Kulai and Johor Bahru.
In Sarawak, MetMalaysia issued warnings for Serian; Samarahan (Simunjan); Sri Aman (Sri Aman); Betong; Sarikei; Sibu (Sibu and Kanowit); Mukah (Tanjung Manis and Daro); and Kapit (Song). In Sabah, the affected areas are the West Coast (Penampang, Kota Kinabalu and Tuaran) and Sandakan (Kinabatangan).
MetMalaysia said the warning was issued when there were signs of thunderstorms with rainfall intensity exceeding 20mm per hour, which are expected to occur or persist for more than an hour.
"Thunderstorm warnings are short-term alerts valid for no more than six hours per issuance," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Samaiden completes solar PV project for Nippon Electric Glass, strengthens EPCC capabilities
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Renewable energy specialist Samaiden Group Bhd has completed a high-complexity solar photovoltaic (PV) installation for Nippon Electric Glass (Malaysia) Sdn Bhd (NEGM), reinforcing its engineering, procurement, construction and commissioning (EPCC) capabilities for large corporations.
In a statement today, Samaiden said the project comprises rooftop solar PV and building-integrated photovoltaic (BIPV) systems, demonstrating a scalable and practical approach to renewable energy adoption.
"The project represents an important step in the company's efforts to strengthen environmental responsibility while enhancing long-term energy efficiency," it said.
Samaiden added that with the system, NEGM is expected to reduce approximately 6,900 tonnes of carbon dioxide (CO2) emissions annually, reinforcing its commitment to more sustainable operations and a lower-carbon future.
The completion also underscores the positive partnership between Samaiden and NEGM in bringing the project to completion.
NEGM's managing director, Ken Hamajima, said the solar PV installation marks an important step forward for the company as it continues advancing its environmental, social, and governance (ESG) journey and taking real action to reduce its carbon footprint.
"It reflects our commitment to building a more sustainable business and ensuring that our growth is aligned with greater environmental responsibility," he said.
Meanwhile, Samaiden group managing director Datuk Chow Pui Hee said the project demonstrates how companies are translating sustainability ambitions into actionable initiatives.
"We are pleased to have supported NEGM in delivering this project, which combines both rooftop solar PV and BIPV as part of a practical clean energy solution.
"It is encouraging to see more companies translating their sustainability aspirations into visible and actionable initiatives," she said.
Samaiden said the project further positioned the company as a trusted solutions provider to corporations seeking to implement impactful, scalable renewable energy initiatives.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Proposal to bring home elephants Dara, Amoi, Kelat must be considered – Arthur
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Proposals by various parties, including non-governmental organisations (NGOs), to bring home three Malaysian elephants — Dara, Amoi and Kelat — should be given serious consideration, says Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup.
The three elephants were previously transferred to Tennoji Zoo in Osaka, Japan under a conservation collaboration, a move that has recently drawn public concern over their welfare.
He said if the implementation of the transfer agreement was found to be no longer aligned with conservation principles, animal welfare standards and current sensitivities, consideration should be given to returning the animals to their natural habitat.
After reviewing a memorandum submitted on the matter, the Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Ministry is, in principle, of the view that the proposal to bring the elephants home should be seriously considered, taking into account public interest and current sensitivities.
"In this regard, I strongly urge the management of Zoo Taiping & Night Safari (ZTNS) to initiate discussions with Tennoji Zoo in Osaka to assess the requirements and mechanism for bringing the elephants back, as they are the parties with contractual obligations," he said at a press conference here today.
Arthur said that on grounds of conservation and national interest, the ministry was prepared to consider funding support, if required, to facilitate the repatriation of the elephants as part of efforts to preserve the nation's natural heritage.
"Although the previous administration made the decision, as the minister holding the mandate today, I will not compromise on issues concerning wildlife welfare," he said.
He said the ministry took seriously the concerns over the welfare of the three elephants following videos and images circulating on social media that had drawn widespread public attention.
He added that he had met Japanese Ambassador to Malaysia Noriyuki Shikata on April 13, as well as Taiping Municipal Council president Mohamed Akmal Dahalan and ZTNS management on April 21, to seek clarification on the conservation programme between ZTNS and Tennoji Zoo.
"During the meetings, we put forward several proposals and requested that certain actions be taken and explanations provided publicly, but to date these requests have yet to be fulfilled by both parties," he said.
Arthur said records from the Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan) and checks with ZTNS showed that the collaboration between the two zoos was formalised through agreements dated May 19, 2022 and Oct 6, 2022.
"The ministry is not a signatory to these agreements. The transfer of the three elephants was agreed directly between ZTNS and Tennoji Zoo on a business-to-business (B2B) basis," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Claims of Umno assemblymen rejoining state government false, says Jalaluddin
Article:
SEREMBAN: Umno Negeri Sembilan chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias has denied rumours that Umno state assemblymen have returned to hold positions in the Negri Sembilan state executive council (exco).
He described the claims as baseless and untrue.
"That is just a rumour… false," he said briefly when contacted to deny reports published in a local newspaper today.
Lenggeng assemblyman Datuk Asna Amin, who is also a former deputy speaker of the Negri Sembilan state legislative assembly, said Umno representatives in the state have not received any instructions from the party leadership.
He said any further action would depend on directives from Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
"We in the state Umno leadership have not received any instructions from the central leadership. As loyal party members, we will adhere to any directives from him (Zahid), who is also BN chairman.
"As long as there are no instructions to return to the exco, these rumours will remain just that," he said here today.
Seri Menanti assemblyman Muhammad Sufian Maradzi also denied the speculation, stressing that he would abide by any decision from the party leadership.
"That is only speculation. So far, we have not received any instructions from the central leadership," he said.
Earlier, social media posts claimed that several Umno assemblymen would be appointed as state exco members, replacing four representatives who had automatically vacated their positions after withdrawing support for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun.
Apart from Asna and Sufian, Jeram Padang assemblyman Datuk Zaidy Abdul Kadir and Senaling assemblyman Datuk Ismail Lasim were also linked to the rumours.
Previously, all 14 Umno-BN assemblymen withdrew their support for Aminuddin, citing a loss of confidence in the state government led by the Menteri Besar.
Meanwhile, Aminuddin, speaking at a press conference at Wisma Negeri today, said he had no information regarding the matter.
"It is not appropriate for me to deny the report as I actually have no information about it," he said.
He also said that no negotiations had taken place between PH and BN or PN following the political impasse.
He said since the 14 Umno-BN assemblymen withdrew their support, there had been no further discussions among the parties involved.
"I tried to contact several Umno assemblymen on Monday morning after learning about the matter, but the calls did not go through.
"There have been no discussions with BN assemblymen to regain support because they have already withdrawn their backing. They do not want to work with me.
"Whether they want to return to support or not, I need approval from my party president and cannot act unilaterally," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Nine marriages, 11 children: Suspect in miscarriage assault was married 9 times, has 11 children
Article:
ALOR STAR: The 43-year-old man suspected of assaulting his wife in Sungai Petani until she suffered a miscarriage had been married nine times and has 11 children.
Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said all of the suspect's marriages involved women who were civil servants in several states.
He said preliminary investigations found the suspect currently has three wives, while six others were former wives.
Earlier today, the suspect,  Rosmaini Abd Raof, 43,, was remanded for a week for assaulting his wife in Ambangan Heights in Sungai Petani on April 23.
Adzli said initial report was lodged by the victim's 45-year-old brother, who was informed by the hospital that his sister had allegedly been assaulted by her husband.
"Following the report, police opened an investigation under Section 323 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing hurt.
"On April 26, the victim lodged a report herself and complained of severe pain.
"A medical examination found she had sustained serious injuries, including fractures to her left and right ribs and spine. She also suffered a miscarriage," he said during a press conference at the Kedah police contingent headquarters here today (Wednesday).
Adzli said following the medical report, police reclassified the case under Section 326 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing grievous hurt which carries a maximum penalty of 20 years in jail, a fine, or whipping upon conviction.
He said the victim claimed she was repeatedly assaulted using several objects, including metal rods, a hanger, a hose, a curtain rod and a broom handle.
Adzli said the suspect was the same individual involved in a previous case in Johor Baru, where he was convicted of assaulting his wife and sentenced to 10 years in prison.
He said the suspect was in the process of appealing the conviction and was out on RM7,000 court bail.
In that case, Rosmaini was accused of assaulting his wife who was also pregnant at the time, at an apartment in Larkin, Johor, resulting in severe injuries, including a brain haemorrhage that required part of her skull to be removed, as well as fractures to her jaw, spine and ribs.
Now aged 48, the wife remains bedridden, although she safely delivered a baby boy in November 2021.
"Once the suspect was identified, we formed two teams from the Kuala Muda district police headquarters and the Kedah police contingent headquarters to track him down.
"He was arrested at a homestay in this city (Alor Star) at 4.45pm yesterday," he said.
He said police also seized four mobile phones and a Mercedes-Benz car.
Adzli said the suspect had four previous criminal records involving assault and criminal intimidation against his wife and girlfriend.
He said the suspect was also involved in a 2018 case of concealing a birth, which is still being tried in Alor Star.
Adzli said preliminary investigations found the suspect's latest marriage, involving the victim, had taken place in a neighbouring country.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MCMC's Nadi joins UN telecom agency's global digital network
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The National Information Dissemination Centre (Nadi) under the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has been recognised as the 16th global implementing partner of the International Telecommunication Union (ITU).
Government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the cabinet was briefed on Nadi's achievement during its meeting today.
"Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi also proposed that the rural community centres under the Rural and Regional Development Ministry streamline activities with Nadi in the near future," he said in his weekly post-cabinet meeting media briefing.
On April 25, Fahmi said Nadi had officially been
accepted as a member
of the ITU Digital Transformation Centres network.
He said the recognition demonstrated that community-level initiatives could deliver significant impact and receive international acknowledgement.
Fahmi added that 1,099 Nadi centres were currently operating nationwide, with a membership of 1.9 million, reflecting strong public acceptance and its role as a key platform in helping communities adapt to economic and technological change.
MCMC previously said the membership also opened up opportunities for globally recognised digital certification, enhancing the marketability of local talent in the digital economy.
The commission said the recognition underscored Malaysia's commitment to expanding digital inclusion, particularly among rural communities and those with limited access to technology.
Meanwhile, Fahmi said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had stressed the need for closer coordination in implementing the Kampung Angkat Madani programme.
He said the prime minister also emphasised that the initiative was a federal-level effort and must be properly aligned, particularly with members of parliament from among government supporters and backbenchers.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rising star of France's far right weaponised a routine inquiry against public broadcasters
Article:
A French fact-finding inquiry — normally a dull parliamentary ritual — has turned into a polarising political spectacle, giving France's far right an unexpected platform to wage a culture-war campaign against public broadcasters.
Leading the probe is 32-year-old Charles Alloncle, an ally of National Rally leader Jordan Bardella, who has transformed it into a gripping confrontation between far-right forces in parliament and institutions they have long accused of bias.
By aggressively grilling prominent journalists, star presenters and senior executives - and circulating viral excerpts on social media - Alloncle has drawn unusual public attention and emerged as a rising star of the far right.
As the six-month inquiry into France's 4-billion-euro-a-year public broadcasters draws to a close, media analysts and lawmakers say its impact will outlast the hearings themselves.
They warn the probe has helped erode faith in public broadcasting and laid the groundwork for the National Rally's (RN) pledge to privatise the sector if it wins power in 2027 elections.
RN leaders have relished turning the spotlight on public broadcasters that have long scrutinised the far right and its media allies.
For Alloncle, who currently sits with a group of breakaway conservatives aligned with the RN, the reward may be a cabinet post in a future far-right government, RN sources said.
"He really did the job," RN lawmaker Renault Matthias told Reuters. "It's part of a culture war. Privatising public broadcasting is in our manifesto, so this serves our programme."
Critics say the inquiry has gone well beyond standard parliamentary oversight, drawing comparisons to attacks by Britain's right-wing parties on the BBC before and after the Brexit referendum.
While early questioning focused on alleged editorial bias, the emphasis shifted to costs, using selective examples and unsupported insinuation to weaken public support for state-funded media, they say.
"The strategy is not so much to debate editorial bias, but to hammer home the message that it 'costs too much'," said Erwan Balanant, a centrist lawmaker on the committee.
"It's a very effective way of destroying the legitimacy of public broadcasting."
Alloncle cited a 60,000 euro fee paid to actress Virginie Efira to host the Cannes Film Festival opening ceremony in 2022 - not an unusual sum by international standards - as an example of what he said was profligate spending by public broadcasters.
He also revealed the overall amount paid by the public broadcaster to secure the festival's broadcast rights — commercially sensitive information that private competitors could use to undercut future bids.
[[nid:1429033]]
At the outset of the inquiry in December, polling showed 70 per cent of French people had a good impression of public broadcasting.
But Alloncle's budget-focused message has resonated at a time of cost-of-living pressures.
When Reuters spoke to Alloncle near parliament, a passer-by stopped to congratulate him. "Keep up the good work," the man said.
Alloncle told Reuters he entered politics from the finance sector after meeting Bardella at a technology event. He was later installed as a candidate under an electoral pact between the RN and a smaller coalition partner.
He made no apology for his combative style, which he said reflects a toughness forged in business rather than politics. He said his sole aim was upholding French media laws that guarantee a plurality of political views.
"That means making sure public broadcasters do not chronically under-represent the RN, but also, say, the hard-left party France Unbowed," he said.
Alloncle's message has been amplified by the media empire of conservative Catholic billionaire Vincent Bollore, who could benefit from any privatisation of state media.
His outlets - including CNews, Journal du Dimanche and Europe 1 - have given extensive coverage to the inquiry and to Alloncle's appearances.
Some lawmakers allege Bollore's media arm, the Lagardère News holding, went further - accusing it of circulating lists of suggested questions reflecting what they described as a hostile line toward public broadcasters.
"It was a clear case of interference, and I told them to stop," the inquiry's chair, centrist lawmaker Jeremie Patrier-Leitus told Reuters, backing up an earlier report by Le Monde.
Lagardère News and Alloncle did not respond to a request for comment on the list of questions.
For media historian Alexis Levrier, the episode illustrates a broader convergence between media ownership and political power. "It's a political and media empire aspiring to wield power in 2027," he said.
Testifying before the same inquiry last month, Bollore denied any coordination with Alloncle.
"I had never seen him before today," Bolloré told lawmakers. "But he seems very likeable — and very effective."
* The writer is from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nephew and wife remanded over alleged RM15k jewellery robbery
Article:
BATU PAHAT: An elderly couple were shocked to discover that the man believed to be their nephew robbed them of a gold bracelet worth about RM15,000 after breaking into their home last week.
The 71-year-old victim was at home with her husband, in his 80s, when they were confronted by a masked man in the incident at about 1pm on April 20.
At first, the victim thought the suspect was someone else as he was wearing a mask.
The man, who worked at a factory, then took the jewellery and fled with his 31-year-old wife, who had been waiting in a car parked not far from the house.
He had initially demanded that the victim hand over a necklace. However, she was not wearing one at the time, so he instead took her bracelet.
At the Magistrates' Court here today, the suspect and his wife were remanded for 7 days until next Tuesday.
Magistrate Nurasidah A Rahman granted the remand order to allow police to investigate the case under Section 395 of the Penal Code and record statements from both suspects.
The offence carries imprisonment of up to 20 years and whipping upon conviction.
Earlier, a police team from the Special Investigation/Serious Crimes Division (D9) of the Batu Pahat district police headquarters launched an investigation and arrested both suspects at their home in Klang on Tuesday afternoon.
The arrests were made after police traced the vehicle used in the incident, with the help of closed-circuit television footage.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: DPS, Xingfengwei to explore AGI data centre projects worth over RM4bil
Article:
MELAKA: Melaka is set to strengthen its position as a leading regional digital hub as DPS Resources Bhd (DPS) explores the development of artificial general intelligence (AGI) data centre infrastructure projects here with an indicative value of about US$1.1 billion or over RM4 billion (US$1=RM3.95).
This strategic step is being realised through its wholly owned subsidiary, DPS Energy Sdn Bhd (DPSE), which signed a memorandum of understanding (MoU) with Hangzhou Xinfengwei Network Technology Co Ltd (Xinfengwei) yesterday.
In a statement issued today, Bursa Malaysia-listed DPS said Xinfengwei is an associated company of Dingding (China) Information Technology Co Ltd, which is an affiliate entity within Alibaba Group Holding Ltd, a company listed on the New York Stock Exchange and Hong Kong Stock Exchange.
DPS group chairman and founder Tan Sri Dr Sow Chin Chuan said the MoU represents an important step in DPS' strategy to unlock the long-term potential of its landbank and infrastructure capabilities in Melaka.
"By collaborating with Xinfengwei, we aim to explore opportunities in AGI data centre infrastructure, where demand for high-performance computing capacity continues to grow globally," he said.
Sow said that although the MoU is not binding at this stage, it serves as a meaningful foundation for both parties to evaluate commercial structures, technical requirements, and potential offtake arrangements.
"Our immediate focus is to advance discussions towards definitive agreements in a disciplined manner, ensuring that any future development is supported by sound commercial terms, regulatory compliance, and long-term value creation for DPS and its shareholders," he said.
Under the proposed collaboration, DPSE will provide land, power supply and supporting infrastructure for the project sites, while overseeing regulatory approvals, government liaison, compliance requirements, and overall project management in Malaysia.
Xinfengwei is expected to introduce global computing demand and potential customers, as well as undertake market development, technical operations, and day-to-day business management.
According to the statement, both parties aim to achieve definitive agreements for the first phase of the project, involving a capacity of about 150 megawatts (MW) to 180MW, with estimated development costs of around US$7.5 million per MW.
Based on a 150MW capacity, the project value is estimated to reach US$1.1 billion, subject to confirmation and final agreed terms.
The statement noted that as artificial intelligence continues to reshape industries and drive demand for large-scale computing capacity, data centre infrastructure is becoming a critical foundation of the digital economy.
"As a landowner with infrastructure development capabilities in Melaka, DPS is well-positioned to unlock the value of its land bank through AGI data centre developments, with the potential to establish long-term recurring income streams upon successful execution of the definitive agreements," it said.
DOPS added that the proposed collaboration also reflects its ambition to play a role in attracting high-value technology investments into Melaka, thereby supporting the state's emergence as a growing hub for digital infrastructure hub and contributing to its broader economic development.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: French teen in straw licking case allowed to leave Singapore
Article:
SINGAPORE: A French teen facing criminal charges in Singapore for a straw licking stunt was granted permission on Wednesday to leave the country for three weeks on pledges to return.
The 18-year-old is accused of posting to social media a video of himself putting the straw he licked back into the dispenser on an orange juice vending machine.
Didier Gaspard Owen Maximilien's clip went viral and triggered a backlash that led to his arrest in Singapore, which has a reputation for not tolerating bad behaviour.
The teen's lawyer sought permission from a judge for him to travel to Manila from May 2-25 for an internship, a key requirement for him to graduate.
The judge granted the request after the prosecution posed no objection, but asked that he must remain contactable while overseas and required a SG$5,000 (US$3,900) bond.
His next appearance in the Singapore court was also rescheduled from May 22 to May 29.
The teen, who is studying in Singapore and is out on bail, was charged last Friday over the straw stunt.
He uploaded the video on Instagram knowing that it "would or would probably cause annoyance to the public", according to court documents.
The public nuisance offence carries a jail term of up to three months and a fine.
A second charge of committing mischief said Maximilien knew that he was "likely to cause wrongful loss or damage" to iJooz, the company operating the vending machine which had to replace all 500 straws in the dispenser.
The mischief offence carries a punishment of up to two years in jail on conviction and a fine, according to the charge sheet.
Both offences were allegedly committed on March 12.
The Straits Times newspaper said the video "quickly went viral, sparking shock and concern among netizens."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Short-term interbank rates end steady following Bank Negara operations
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Short-term interbank rates ended steady today following Bank Negara Malaysia's operations to absorb surplus liquidity from the financial system.
Liquidity in the conventional system eased to RM40.84 billion from RM42.43 billion this morning, while liquidity in Islamic funds decreased to RM30.38 billion from RM32.51 billion.
Earlier, the central bank conducted a reverse repo tender.
At 4pm, it called a RM40.80 billion conventional money market tender and a RM30.40 billion murabahah money market tender, both for one-day funds.
The Malaysia Islamic Overnight Rate (MYOR-i) stood at 2.75 per cent as at April 28, 2026.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fake account not ruled out in viral image insulting Selangor Sultan, says Fahmi
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Authorities have not ruled out the possibility that a fake account was used to upload a viral image allegedly edited to insult the Sultan of Selangor, says Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil.
Fahmi, who is also the government spokesman, said a man had had his statement recorded by the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) but had denied uploading the content.
He said the MCMC was working with Meta and the police to carry out forensic analysis to verify the claims made by the person.
"Among the matters currently under investigation is whether there is an element of a fake account or an agent provocateur deliberately creating an account to upload content that then provokes other parties, in addition to inciting public anger," he told a press conference here today.
He said investigations were ongoing and further updates would be provided by the police and MCMC.
"But I can confirm that as of today, the possibility that a fake account was created to upload content of a 3R nature has not been ruled out," he said.
On Monday, the Selangor Royal Council lodged a
police report
over an offensive edited image posted on social media insulting the Sultan of Selangor.
Federal police Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said investigations had been launched into a Facebook account owner.
He said 21 witnesses, including complainants, have been called to have their
statements recorded
to assist investigations.
The case is being investigated under Section 4(1) of the Sedition Act 1948, which covers acts with a seditious tendency and carries a fine of up to RM5,000, imprisonment of up to three years, or both.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zurich units hit with penalities over sanction failures: Bank Negara
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia has imposed administrative monetary penalties of RM1.04 million on Zurich General Insurance Malaysia Bhd (ZGIMB) and RM520,000 on Zurich General Takaful Malaysia Bhd (ZGTMB) for non-compliance with targeted financial sanction (TFS) requirements on Jan 19.
In a statement today, the central bank said that, as reporting institutions (RIs), Zurich entities are required to update their sanctions database without delay upon publication of the Domestic List.
It said that all RIs must comply with TFS requirements and that it will not hesitate to take supervisory and/or enforcement action against those that fail to meet legal and regulatory obligations.
They should also conduct sanctions screening on existing, prospective and new customers against both the Domestic List and the United Nations Security Council Resolutions List as part of customer due diligence.
RIs must also ascertain potential matches and make further inquiries to determine whether such matches are true matches.
"Upon determination of any true match, RIs are required to freeze the customer's funds and submit a report to Bank Negara and the Royal Malaysia Police immediately. This is essential to protect RIs and the broader financial system from being abused for terrorism financing activities," the central bank said.
Bank Negara said the breaches were identified based on Zurich's reports on the onboarding of several specified entities listed in the Domestic List as customers, arising from its failure to promptly update its sanctions database, which led to screening being conducted against outdated information.
It added that Zurich also failed to ascertain potential matches and undertake further inquiries to determine whether these were true matches.
Additionally, ZGIMB failed to freeze funds and report to Bank Negara immediately upon determining that it was in possession or control of funds linked to one specified entity.
The central bank said the breaches were attributed to gaps in Zurich's sanctions screening systems and standard operating procedures, as well as insufficient staff oversight and awareness.
Zurich has since taken remedial measures, including enhancements to its procedures and refresher training for relevant staff to strengthen compliance with TFS requirements.
In determining the penalties, Bank Negara said it considered aggravating and mitigating factors, including the severity of the breaches, lack of reasonable care in ensuring compliance, past compliance record, and post-misconduct conduct, including the effectiveness of remedial actions.
ZGIMB paid RM1.04 million and ZGTMB Zurich General Takaful Malaysia Bhd RM520,000 on Jan 26, in respect of the penalties imposed.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Philippine VP faces impeachment vote in Congress after panel find probable cause
Article:
MANILA: The Philippine House justice committee on Wednesday found probable cause to impeach Vice President Sara Duterte, moving a step closer to a process that could derail her bid to become the next president.
The complaint will go to the plenary for a vote when Congress resumes session next month, with the backing of a third of its members needed to advance to an impeachment trial in the Senate, where if convicted she would be removed from office and banned from politics for life.
Duterte's impeachment was sought by civil society and leftist groups, who accuse her of misusing public funds, accumulating unexplained wealth and of threatening the lives of her rival, President Ferdinand Marcos Jr, his wife and his cousin, the former house speaker.
In a vote of the committee, all 53 members agreed there was probable cause to impeach Duterte. She denies wrongdoing and says the complaint is politically motivated.
Duterte remains locked in an acrimonious public feud with former ally Marcos, with whom she ran on a joint ticket in 2022 elections that they both won in landslides.
With Marcos limited by the constitution to a single term in office, Duterte is the clear favourite to succeed him in 2028.
Announcing her decision in February to run for the top job, Duterte apologised for helping Marcos to become president.
The vice president's lawyer did not immediately respond to a request for comment on the house committee's decision.
Duterte has previously dismissed the complaint as "just a piece of paper."
The vice president has been subjected to intense scrutiny since the fallout with Marcos and her latest battle comes as her father, former President Rodrigo Duterte, prepares to go on trial at the International Criminal Court in The Hague over thousands of killings during his signature "war on drugs."
Sara Duterte could become the second most senior office holder to be impeached in the Philippines after former President Joseph Estrada, whose 2001 Senate trial was aborted when some prosecutors walked out.
Three other top officials have been impeached and include an ombudsman and an election commission head who both resigned before their trials, and former Supreme Court chief justice Renato Corona, who was the only one convicted so far.
A previous attempt to impeach Duterte on similar allegations was backed overwhelmingly by a Congress stacked with allies of Marcos but it was thrown out in July last year by the Supreme Court, which ruled it was unconstitutional due to a procedural flaw.
Marcos survived a separate impeachment bid in February after his allies in Congress voted to dismiss it.
He has distanced himself from the proceedings against Duterte
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US lawmakers to grill Pentagon chief Hegseth over Iran war
Article:
WASHINGTON: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth will face tough questions from lawmakers about the Iran war on Wednesday during his first testimony to Congress since the start of the conflict.
Hegseth's appearance before the House Armed Services Committee – for a hearing on President Donald Trump's US$1.5 trillion defence budget request – comes with the war still unresolved and the economic fallout from it continuing around the globe.
Lawmakers from both parties have previously expressed dissatisfaction with the information provided in classified briefings on the war, setting up a potentially fiery public hearing in which top US military officer General Dan Caine is also set to testify.
"Finally, Secretary Hegseth will come before the House Armed Services Committee this week. It is time to answer for this war of choice," Representative Maggie Goodlander, a Democratic member of the committee, said in a post on X.
Trump and his administration have yet to publicly provide a plan for winding down the war, which has led Iran to close the vital Strait of Hormuz waterway, sending oil prices skyrocketing.
Washington hit back with a blockade of Iran's ports, and now has three aircraft carriers deployed in the Middle East for the first time in more than 20 years.
- 'MISLED THE PUBLIC' -
Trump has indefinitely extended what was initially a two-week ceasefire, but negotiations have yet to yield a breakthrough.
House Democrats introduced six articles of impeachment against Hegseth earlier this month, accusing him of "high crimes and misdemeanours" including waging war on Iran without congressional approval.
More than a dozen Democrats also sent a letter to Hegseth last week demanding a "formal, immediate investigation" into the deaths of six US troops in Kuwait on March 1, saying the Pentagon chief failed to protect American forces and then "misled the public about the circumstances of the attack."
A total of 13 American troops have been killed in the conflict – six in the Iranian attack in Kuwait, one in another attack in Saudi Arabia, and six in a plane crash in Iraq – while 400 have been wounded.
In addition to the Iran war, Hegseth may face questions about repeated shakeups of senior Pentagon personnel since Trump returned to office last year.
The Pentagon announced last week that Navy Secretary John Phelan was leaving office "immediately," a move that followed the removal of top US Army officer General Randy George earlier in April.
Lawmakers may also raise Hegseth's conflict with artificial intelligence firm Anthropic, which refused to allow its AI models to be used for mass surveillance of civilians or in fully autonomous lethal military operations.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Court orders govt to pay RM1mil over fatal fallen tree in Kuala Terengganu
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court has rejected the government's attempt to use the "act of God" defence in a lawsuit involving a fallen tree that killed one man and seriously injured two others in Kuala Terengganu.
Judicial Commissioner Yusrin Faidz Yusoff ruled the Public Works Department (PWD) and the federal government were fully liable for the 2017 incident and ordered them to pay over RM1 million in damages.
In his grounds of judgment dated April 19, Yusrin ruled that the defence failed because there was no evidence of extraordinary or unforeseeable natural forces.
"There was no evidence of extraordinary weather or any overwhelming natural event that could justify the sudden collapse of the tree without human fault. The defence that it was an 'act of God' cannot be sustained," he said.
The case stemmed from a tragedy on Oct 7, 2017, along Jalan Mengabang Lekor, Batu Rakit, when a large tree suddenly fell onto two passing vehicles.
A motorvan driver was killed, while a woman and her mother, travelling in a car, sustained severe, life-altering injuries.
The victims and the estate of the deceased initiated legal action, naming the PWD, the government, and Roadcare (M) Sdn Bhd among the defendants, alleging negligence in the maintenance and upkeep of the roadside tree within the federal road reserve.
However, the Sessions Court dismissed their claims, finding no liability on the part of the defendants and rejecting the suit in its entirety.
Dissatisfied with the outcome, the plaintiffs filed an appeal, arguing that the lower court had erred in its findings on duty of care and causation.
The High Court has now overturned that decision, reinstating liability against PWD and the government while clearing Roadcare.
Yusrin held that PWD and the government, as the authorities in control of the federal road reserve, owed a duty of care to ensure the safety of road users.
"The land is under the exclusive purview of the federal government, exercised through its agency, the PWD.
"The duty of care rests with the authority that owns and controls the land from which the hazard originates," he said.
He found that the Sessions Court had erred in law and fact by absolving the department and the government while attempting to shift liability to the Kuala Terengganu City Council (MBKT), which had already been struck out from the suit.
"It was illogical and an error of law to place responsibility on a party that had already been judicially determined not to be the proper defendant," he said.
The judge also pointed to the absence of any proper system to monitor roadside trees.
"There was no evidence of any inspection regime.
"The authorities failed to show that reasonable steps had been taken to identify or mitigate the potential danger posed by trees within the road reserve.
"A massive, mature tree collapsing onto a public road is not an event that ordinarily occurs in the absence of negligence. In the ordinary course of nature, healthy trees do not simply fall without prior signs of disease, decay, or instability," he added.
He said the defendants produced no evidence of inspection, no expert testimony, or any explanation consistent with the exercise of reasonable care.
However, the court upheld the finding that Roadcare was not liable, noting that its concession agreement expressly excluded responsibility for landscaping and tree maintenance.
The court awarded RM265,052 in the fatal claim and RM751,770 to the injured victims, and ordered the defendants to pay costs.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Finance Ministry: Expenditure cuts will not disrupt critical public services
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Expenditure adjustments by ministries and government agencies will be carried out without affecting critical public services or economic stability, the Finance Ministry said.
In a statement today, the ministry said agencies have been directed to restructure their operating expenditure priorities in response to pressures from the global supply crisis and rising subsidy costs.
It said the move is part of a prudent fiscal management approach to optimise government resources and ensure continued support for the people.
"This step is aimed at ensuring government resources are managed efficiently so that assistance to the rakyat can be sustained on an ongoing basis," it said.
Government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said today that the
move
aligns with the government's plan to rationalise programme activities and implementation amid global supply challenges.
Fahmi had said that Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, during today's Cabinet meeting, stressed that all official events, workshops, and large-scale programmes must be conducted on a moderate scale.
The directive applies to all ministries, agencies, statutory bodies, government-linked companies (GLCs), and government-linked investment companies (GLICs).
Reuters reported today that the Treasury had instructed all ministries and agencies to reduce their operating budgets this year, citing the impact of the ongoing Middle East conflict.
According to a directive dated today and issued by Treasury secretary-general Datuk Johan Mahmood Merican, a sharp increase in energy prices stemming from the conflict had directly affected the cost of living.
The document stated that the government's subsidy bill was projected to reach RM58.4 billion this year, significantly higher than the RM15 billion initially allocated under the 2026 Budget.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran takes seized ships to port, countries seek info on seafarers' safety
Article:
LONDON: Two container ships seized by Iran near the Strait of Hormuz with about 40 crew aboard have been taken toward the port of Bandar Abbas, sources said on Thursday, Teheran having vowed to retaliate after US forces seized an Iranian vessel three days earlier.
Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps seized the ships on Wednesday, one operated by MSC, the world's biggest container shipping group, and according to three sources, the other chartered by the firm.
"Some 20 Iranians armed to the teeth stormed the ship. Sailors are under Iranians' control, their movements on the ship are limited, but the Iranians are treating them well," a relative of one of the seafarers involved told Reuters.
"The ship is anchored nine nautical miles from the Iranian coast. Negotiations between MSC and Iran are ongoing, our sailors are fine," Montenegro's minister of maritime affairs, Filip Radulovic, told state broadcaster state RTCG.
Four sailors on the MSC Francesca including its captain are from Montenegro, he said. Two Croatians are also aboard, Croatia's foreign ministry confirmed.
Full details on the crew of the Panama-flagged ship have not been made available, but large container vessels normally require a minimum of about 20. MSC has declined to comment.
The Liberia-flagged Epaminondas has a crew of 21 members made up of Ukrainians and Filipinos, according to the Greek coast guard. It had been bound for India.
Both crews were reported safe, but authorities in their home countries said they were seeking information about the well-being of the seafarers and working on their release.
There has been no information released about what, if any, cargo the ships were carrying.
Both ships had their tracking transponder systems switched off but maritime security sources said shipping data suggested they were near Bandar Abbas.
On April 19, US forces fired upon and seized the Iran-flagged Touska cargo ship.
"The armed forces of the Islamic Republic of Iran will soon respond and retaliate against this armed piracy by the U.S. military," a military spokesperson said in response.
Iran's foreign ministry demanded the immediate release of the vessel, its crew and their families. No details about its crew have been released.
Oil prices have jumped on dimmed prospects for a quick reopening of the strait, a route which usually handles 20% of the world's daily oil and liquefied natural gas supply.
Benchmark Brent crude oil futures were up two per cent at US$104 a barrel on Thursday versus US$72 before the war began on Feb 28.
US Central Command said on Wednesday that since the start of its blockade against ships entering or exiting Iranian ports, US forces had directed 31 vessels to turn around or return to port.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Father pleads guilty to raping daughter; victim found 18 weeks pregnant
Article:
BATU PAHAT: A 47-year-old labourer faces up to 30 years' imprisonment and a minimum of 10 strokes of the rotan after pleading guilty in the Magistrate's Court here today to raping his daughter, who was later found to be 18 weeks pregnant.
The father of four entered the plea to two charges read before Magistrate Arun Noval Dass.
According to the charges, the accused raped his 20-year-old daughter in his living room and bedroom here between March last year and January this year.
He was charged under Section 376(3) of the Penal Code.
Deputy public prosecutor Firdaus Ruslan appeared for the prosecution, while the accused was unrepresented.
As the case falls under the jurisdiction of the Sessions Court, the magistrate fixed tomorrow for the mention of facts and sentencing. No bail was offered following the guilty plea.
It was reported that the victim had complained of stomach pain and was taken to a health clinic by family members.
Medical examinations later revealed she was pregnant.
The victim was subsequently referred to Sultanah Nora Ismail Hospital, where she disclosed the abuse and claimed the accused had threatened her into silence.
Investigations found the incidents occurred at the family home, where the victim was forced to comply with the accused's demands.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police nab trio over brutal hacking death of man in Tangkak
Article:
TANGKAK: Three men believed to be the main suspects in the brutal daylight parang attack on a man in Bukit Kangkar last month have been arrested after more than a month on the run.
The suspects, aged between 24 and 32, were detained at three separate locations last night following intelligence gathering and public tip-offs.
District police chief Superintendent Roslan Mohd Talib confirmed the arrests.
"We have detained three suspects. We are confident these arrests will help resolve the murder case," he said when contacted.
The magistrate's court here today granted a seven-day remand order for the trio, effective until next Tuesday.
Magistrate Lee Kim Keat issued the order to facilitate investigations under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder.
Earlier, police detained 10 people to assist in investigations into the March 13 incident, which occurred at 7.40pm. However, all were later released on police bail after their statements were recorded.
In the incident, 38-year-old Tong Wei Sheng
died
shortly after arriving at hospital due to severe injuries to his arms, legs and back.
Tong was reportedly chased by three men armed with parangs near a restaurant before attempting to take refuge at a house in Batu 9, Bukit Kangkar.
Investigations revealed that the victim and a friend had gone to the location to meet members of a group before several vehicles arrived.
Closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage showed the victim being pursued by three masked men wearing hoodies and wielding parangs, before the suspects fled the scene in a car after the attack.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Immigration arrests 77 undocumented foreign workers in eateries, packaging plant raids
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Seventy-seven foreign workers were detained in a two-day Immigration enforcement operation after they were found working without valid permits at eateries and a vegetable packaging facility here.
Johor Immigration Department director Datuk Mohd Rusdi Mohd Darus said the operations were carried out following public tip-offs and weeks of intelligence, targeting premises employing undocumented foreign workers and those misusing social visit passes.
On April 27, enforcement teams simultaneously raided several food outlets here, where 39 foreign workers employed as waiters and cooks without valid documentation were detained.
The Immigration Department detained 26 Myanmar citizens, including six women, 11 Bangladeshi citizens, including one woman, and two Indonesian men, aged between 19 and 46.
"A Malaysian man believed to be a human resource manager was also detained to assist investigations," Rusdi said.
A day later, the enforcement team focused its attention on a vegetable packaging facility, where 38 foreigners were detained.
The 37 Indonesians, including 10 women, and one from China, aged between 18 and 54, were found working at the facility but failed to produce valid identification documents and were believed to have misused social visit passes for employment.
The facility's Malaysian manager was also detained for allegedly harbouring illegal immigrants.
"All detainees, currently held at the Setia Tropika Immigration Depot, are being investigated under the Immigration Act 1959/63 and its regulations for overstaying and misuse of social visit passes," Rusdi said in a statement today.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tobacco control council calls for reintroduction of GEG provision in smoking law
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 29 (Bernama) -- The Malaysian Council for Tobacco Control (MCTC) has urged the government to consider reintroducing the Generational End Game (GEG) provision under the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act 2024 (Act 825).
Its president, Prof Dr Murallitharan Munisamy, said the proposal was part of efforts to strengthen protection for future generations amid continuing public health challenges.
He said there was room to consider additional measures such as the GEG policy to complement existing tobacco control initiatives.
"To ensure a balanced and effective approach, we propose a comprehensive review of the GEG, including stronger engagement with stakeholders and a phased implementation that prioritises public education.
"The government can also undertake comparative studies on countries such as the United Kingdom (UK), which has banned the sale of tobacco products to individuals born on or after a specified year," he said in a statement today.
Murallitharan said the approach adopted by the UK was gaining global attention as a sustainable long-term strategy to reduce the burden of smoking-related diseases.
The statement also quoted constitutional law expert Prof Emeritus Dr Shad Saleem Faruqi as saying controls on smoking were consistent with the principles of the Federal Constitution and that there was no absolute right for individuals to smoke or vape.
According to the statement, Shad Saleem said the age-based GEG approach was lawful and acceptable, with the main emphasis on effective implementation.
"This provides a basis for confidence that discussions on the GEG can continue constructively," Murallitharan said.
Provisions related to the GEG were dropped from the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Bill 2023 tabled in the Dewan Rakyat in late November 2023.
The bill was later passed after its second reading by then Health Minister Dr Zaliha Mustafa. — Bernama

Title: Sabah moves to cushion tourism sector as Middle East conflict cuts airline routes
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, April 29 — Sabah’s tourism sector is bracing for turbulence as the ongoing Middle East conflict begins to ripple through global travel, prompting airlines to rationalise routes and forcing the state to rethink its connectivity strategy.
State Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Datuk Jafry Ariffin said airlines have cited profitability and operational concerns linked to the conflict as key reasons for the reduction of direct flights into Sabah, including routes to Kota Kinabalu and Tawau.
“Airlines do prioritise routes based on profitability, and with the current geopolitical situation, they are reviewing frequencies and passenger loads. These are among the reasons given for cutting back flights to Sabah,” he said during a question-and-answer session in the State Legislative Assembly.
He said that airlines represent a structural vulnerability for the state, with more than 90 per cent of tourist arrivals depending on air connectivity.
Jafry said that Sabah entered 2026 on a strong footing as international arrivals rose 17.7 per cent in the first two months of the year compared to the same period in 2025, signalling steady post-pandemic momentum.
However, the global landscape has since shifted, as the Middle East conflict has not only increased operating costs for airlines but also altered travel patterns, particularly for long-haul routes that Sabah relies on to draw higher-spending tourists.
Jafry said his ministry has begun recalibrating its strategy to cushion the impact by engaging with airlines and offering alternatives such as positioning Sabah as a “fly-through” destination — allowing travellers to transit through Kota Kinabalu en route to other regional destinations, potentially offsetting the loss of direct long-haul connectivity.
“As proposed by the Sri Tanjung assemblyman,we are considering options like fly-through arrangements in our engagements with airlines,” Jafry said.
The ministry has pivoted towards strengthening domestic and regional markets as a near-term buffer against global uncertainty.
Campaigns in collaboration with airlines and digital platforms such as Klook, Trip.com and Agoda are being intensified to stimulate local travel demand — a move aligned with federal efforts under Budget 2026 to encourage Malaysians to holiday within the country.
Early signs suggest this strategy is gaining traction. Participation in the Matta Fair in Kuala Lumpur last month generated nearly RM5 million in domestic tourism package sales, reinforcing Sabah’s appeal among local travellers.
Beyond that, the state is doubling down on more resilient regional markets in Southeast Asia and Northeast Asia, seen as less volatile compared to long-haul segments.
“The government is also leaning into event-based tourism — supporting festivals, cultural showcases and industry-led events — to boost visitor numbers and spending, particularly as spontaneous, short-haul travel becomes more prominent,” he said.
Rising fuel costs and broader logistical challenges are pushing up operating expenses, prompting the state to gather data and feedback to assess the scale of impact and consider targeted support measures.
Looking ahead to next year’s Visit Sabah 2027 campaign, Jafry said his ministry is in the midst of planning several short- and medium-term intervention measures to ensure the sustainability of the tourism sector.
“I also welcome the suggestion by the Honourable Member for N.69 to encourage domestic travel through hotel vouchers. This proposal will be brought to the attention of the Sabah Tourism Board as one of the alternative measures to support the industry,” he said.

Title: Sabah faces critical shortage of 4,526 doctors, ratio far below national target
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, April 29 — Sabah is facing a critical shortage of doctors, with the state government saying the gap to the national average is a significant 4,526 doctors across the state.
State Minister for Women, Family and Community Development Datuk Julita Majungki said that there are currently 3,431 government doctors and 1,397 private practitioners in Sabah, resulting in a doctor-to-population ratio of 1:775.
The national target ratio is 1:400.
“Based on our calculations, Sabah requires 9,354 doctors. This means there is currently a shortage of 4,526 doctors compared to existing numbers,” she said during her winding-up speech at the State Legislative Assembly sitting.
Concerns about Sabah’s lack of adequate healthcare facilities have been raised by citizens and opposition leaders highlighting the strain on patients and the existing workforce particularly in rural and underserved areas.
Julita said the shortage of specialists remains a major challenge across hospitals in the state, with ongoing efforts to address the issue through training and incentives.
She said Sabah currently has three cardiologists, six cardiology specialists with fellowship training, two paediatric cardiologists, and one cardiothoracic surgeon.
Among the measures are encouraging medical officers to pursue specialist training, with support from the Health Ministry through increased federal training scholarships specifically for Sabah-based doctors.
“Financial incentives such as the Interior Placement Incentive (BIPP) are offered to attract specialists to Sabah and Sarawak, while the ministry has also appealed for the reinstatement of the Regional Incentive Allowance (BIW), which was previously discontinued by the Public Service Department,” she said.
At the same time, the government is pushing ahead with infrastructure upgrades to ease congestion and improve service delivery.
These include plans to upgrade Hospital Queen Elizabeth II into a cardiac centre, the construction of a new Sabah Blood Centre expected to be completed by 2029, and the procurement of two MRI machines for key hospitals in the state.
Julita also highlighted the completion of the Sabah Dental Specialist Centre in Kota Kinabalu, which is expected to reduce patient load at existing facilities by offering specialised non-hospital-based services.
Separately, the government has outlined a strategic plan to upgrade Hospital Duchess of Kent in Sandakan from a major specialist hospital — currently offering over 20 specialist services — into a full state hospital with more than 49 specialist services.
The upgrade project, approved under the 12th Malaysia Plan (Rolling Plan 4), will include the construction of a new clinical block, an additional 594 patient beds, new wards, and the creation of more positions, including specialist doctors.
Despite the challenges, she stressed that healthcare costs in government facilities remain among the lowest in the country, with Malaysians receiving subsidies of up to 98 per cent of actual treatment costs.

Title: Finance Ministry issues guidelines to curb non‑essential spending, safeguard economy and public services
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, April 29 — The Ministry of Finance today confirmed that guidelines have been issued to ministries and agencies to reprioritise operating expenditure in response to global supply chain challenges and rising subsidy commitments.
The ministry said the measure is part of a prudent fiscal management approach aimed at optimising government resources to ensure continued support for the people.
It stressed that spending adjustments will be carried out without affecting critical public services or undermining economic stability.

Title: Muhyiddin’s trial: Bank officer says Mamfor’s RM19.5m Bersatu donation ‘unusual’ as company sought several RM500,000 cheques on same day
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 29 — Construction company Mamfor Sdn Bhd’s donations totalling RM19.5 million to political party Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia in 2021 and 2022 were unusual because of the way the payments were made, the High Court heard today in Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin’s corruption and money laundering trial.
Bank Muamalat’s PKNS Shah Alam assistant branch manager Norazeanti Miswan said it would not be unusual for an individual, company or institution to make political donations.
But it was unusual in this case, as Bank Muamalat’s customer Mamfor had issued multiple cheques on the same day to the same recipient Bersatu, she said.
“For example, in November 2021, the customer applied for more than one banker’s cheque at one time and paid to the same recipient. Per cheque RM500,000, but in one day more than two or three pieces [of cheques], at one time.
“That’s what I meant, that it appeared frequent and unusual, out of the ordinary. Because normally, customers do not apply for banker’s cheques to be paid to the same recipient through several transactions on the same day,” she said while testifying as the ninth prosecution witness in Muhyiddin’s trial.
Based on the bank’s records, Norazeanti confirmed that Mamfor did not make any donations to other political parties even from 2023 to 2025.
Earlier today, she also confirmed that Mamfor did not make donations to Umno, PAS or DAP from 2020 until the company closed its Bank Muamalat account in 2025, based on bank records.
Earlier today, when asked by Muhyiddin’s lead defence lawyer Datuk Amer Hamzah Arshad, Norazeanti agreed that an “unusual” or “questionable” transaction does not necessarily mean that it is an “unlawful” transaction.
Previously, Norazeanti testified that her branch had flagged all Mamfor’s 29 cheques totalling RM19.5 million to the bank headquarters as unusual due to the frequent and large sums involved, adding that this was done through “questionable activity reports” — otherwise known as the bank’s internal suspicious transaction reports (STR).
She agreed that a transaction cannot be assumed to be “unlawful” just because it was reported internally by her bank branch to Bank Muamalat’s headquarters as a “questionable” transaction.
Besides the donations to Bersatu, she said her bank branch had also filed internal STRs with the bank headquarters on other Mamfor transactions to other recipients.
Norazeanti agreed with Amer that construction is a legitimate business and that it would mean Mamfor had obtained its funds through legitimate activities, also agreeing that the company is entitled to decide how to spend its money.
She agreed with Amer that it would be up to Mamfor to decide to give donations, and that giving donations is generally not an offence.
She agreed that Bank Muamalat could decide not to carry out any transactions for its customers if false information had been provided or if there was anything unlawful, also confirming that the bank had carried out Mamfor’s instructions to send money as donations to Bersatu.
For the RM19.5 million payments to Bersatu, Mamfor had declared them in banking forms as a “donation” or “political donation” to the party.
Norazeanti agreed that the authorities have not taken action against Mamfor for making false declarations.
Norazeanti agreed with Amer that the party Bersatu and its president Muhyiddin are two separate entities, and that a donation to Bersatu does not necessarily mean a donation to Muhyiddin.
Among other things, she confirmed that Mamfor’s cheques were made out to Bersatu instead of Muhyiddin, and that these cheques cannot be described as donations to Muhyiddin.
She confirmed that Mamfor had brought along copies of the company’s letters to Bersatu, when it went to the bank to apply for the banker’s cheques to Bersatu.
Amer showed her these letters, which she agreed were supporting documents and cover letters for the cheques to Bersatu.
Mamfor had addressed those letters to Bersatu’s treasurer, with the letter stating “Sumbangan untuk Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia” (Donation for Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia).
She confirmed that Mamfor’s cheques to Bersatu could only go into the political party’s bank account, as the banks would not process the cheques to be deposited into other recipients’ accounts.
She agreed that the banking system would clearly record all transactions made by cheques and there would be no way for these transactions to be hidden.
She agreed that there are other methods to give donations such as through cash, further agreeing that there is no system to mandatorily record cash donations, unlike donations made through banks.
She agreed that donations through the banking process are more transparent than cash donations, further agreeing that the remittance forms, banker’s cheques and supporting letters showed that Mamfor’s donations to Bersatu were transparent.
She agreed with Amer that Bank Muamalat’s headquarters did not instruct for any of the cheques or payments for the RM19.5 million donations to be withdrawn or to be stopped.
In early 2025, Bank Muamalat had issued a notice for account closure to Mamfor, with the written reason stated as the “contractual agreement between bank and customer in terms of terms and conditions” had not been met, she said.
She confirmed the notice did not mention Mamfor’s donations to Bersatu as a reason for the account closure.
She was unable to confirm if this account closure notice was related to Mamfor’s 2021 and 2022 transactions to Bersatu, saying that only Bank Muamalat’s headquarters would know the reason.
Bank Muamalat PKNS Shah Alam assistant branch manager Norazeanti Miswan, the ninth prosecution witness, is seen leaving the courtroom at the Kuala Lumpur Court Complex following proceedings for the corruption trial April 29, 2026. — Picture by Raymond Manuel
Later when asked by deputy public prosecutor Noralis Mat, Norazeanti explained why she had said Mamfor could choose what to do with the money in its account, saying that bank customers could carry out transactions while bank officers would have the responsibility to report any suspicious transactions.
Asked to elaborate on her agreement that “unusual” transactions are not necessarily “unlawful”, Norazeanti explained that “unusual” transactions would be when it is not for usual situations where a customer pays to suppliers related to the customer’s nature of business.
As for “unlawful”, she said she understood it to mean matters that are not legitimate.
As for why a banker’s cheque is more transparent than a local cheque, she said this is because a customer would need to physically come to a bank branch to apply for banker’s cheques, while the bank would only know when a customer issues a local cheque as the chequebook is kept by the customer.
Companies Commission of Malaysia (SSM) assistant registrar Harshini Radhakrishnan, who testified today as the 10th prosecution witness, confirmed SSM’s records on four companies, namely Mamfor, Nepturis Sdn Bhd, Sutracom Sdn Bhd, and KCJ Engineering Sdn Bhd.
In this trial, Muhyiddin is facing seven charges, namely four counts of alleged power abuse to obtain RM225.3m bribes for Bersatu (from Nepturis Sdn Bhd, Azman Yusoff, Bukhary Equity Sdn Bhd, and RM19.5 million from Mamfor Sdn Bhd) and three counts of alleged money laundering through money that Bersatu received from Bukhary Equity.
The trial before High Court judge Noor Ruwena Md Nurdin will resume on May 25.
Recommended reading:
Muhyiddin’s trial: Mamfor’s RM19.5m ‘donations’ in 29 cheques to Bersatu flagged as suspicious, unusual to give money to political party, banker says

Title: Fahmi: MCMC working with Meta and police to investigate edited Sultan Selangor image case
